quest.26D35A0B06B5EA35.quest_desc.original:
The woman’s walking stick began to burn to cinder as the Four Winds rushed her back to the earth’s embrace. 

She thrust the flame, precious and frail, into a special earthen vessel. There, it was nurtured and grew strong.

As the woman approached the earth once more, the heat of the vessel grew and spread, flowing across the earth. 

Forever after, the cold of Winter would give way to the gentle touch of Spring in an endless dance of seasons.

quest.304838EDEC32DE97.quest_desc.original:
Thank you for playing &n&6Enigmatic Skies&r! We hope you enjoyed your time here!

                    &6&nFurther Reading&r

Inspiration comes from many sources and while there are some portions of this pack that draw on European traditions, it would be fair to say that the pack wouldn’t exist were it not for inspiration drawn from the myths and sacred stories of the native peoples of North America. 

If you’re interested in learning more, please explore the following links:

quest.36A18D327A03810B.quest_desc.original:
The &7Weeping Well&r has been generous with you, you have only one last thing to give it; yourself.

Trust it. Jump in. 
&n                                                  &r

By jumping into the Well, you will receive an &7Umbral Spirit&r. 

If you've lost yours, or simply need more, you can recreate it with some effort in the &7Particle Accelerator&r.

quest.414458433C4734C6.quest_desc.original:
When the woman returned to the Earth, she looked around to see that life was flourishing in the new cycle. The animals, once her teachers and companions, had multiplied and spread across the land. 

The world was blooming, but the woman felt alone again. She had learned much but now had no one with whom to share her knowledge. 

With her hands she dug into the damp earth, molding the clay there into her likeness. With the heat of her fire, she hardened the clay. With the breath in her lungs, she breathed life into the clay. 

With the words in her heart, she taught the new people of the animals and the land. Taught them to hunt and to tend, to plant and to harvest. She taught them to nurture the fires, to create.

quest.5E4077F317135E34.quest_desc.original:
As the woman awoke on the newly formed earth, alone and surrounded by unfamiliar sights. Her belly rumbled and she knew hunger. Her people were gone and she knew fear. She shed tears for what was lost.

The animals, seeing that she hungered, brought her roots to feed her. Seeing that she was alone, they gathered near to comfort her. 

The woman ate her fill and sang her praises to the animals. With her heart full of company, she brushed her tears away. They spread across the sky where they glistened and became stars. With her belly full of nourishment, she pressed the roots into the newly formed earth. They flourished in the soil and multiplied.

As she wandered the new earth, she sang to the animals and they adored her. When her hunger returned, the animals fed her. Each place she visited, she pressed roots into the ground. Each place, they flourished and multiplied. The land grew lush and was filled with new life.

quest.6030BA17DB9228F2.quest_desc.original:
One day the woman found herself in the North and few animals remained with her. 

Here, the land was covered in deep snow and frozen in ice. Here, the woman’s wanderings slowed; she knew she had not the strength of body to traverse the land. She watched as the animals burrowed into the snow to stay warm and she did as they did. 

There she sat, and there she reflected on her travels. 

The North Wind began to blow, strong and cold, and the world stood still.

quest.63A7E92BBCEDF461.quest_desc.original:
All too soon, the woman felt she must move on. She headed into the South and the animals came with her.

Here, forests gave way to wetlands, wetlands to sand-strewn shores. The woman, seeing how the animals swam along the winding waterways, made a canoe to follow them.  When she grew tired, she lay down amongst the sandy dunes of the coast to rest. When she grew unclean, she bathed in the warm waters of the seas. 

Of the animals who accompanied the woman, some found homes in the rivers and seas and were content to stay. 

The South Wind began to blow, hot and strong, bringing with it Summer and the promise of adventure.

quest.6A4DD846F7E08FD6.quest_desc.original:
The woman flew higher and higher, up and up. She soared for what seemed like ages. 

When the air grew thin and her lungs began to burn, the gentle winds of Spring filled her with breath.

When the air grew cold and her limbs began to ache, the warm winds of Summer warmed her flesh. 

Higher and higher, up and up. She soared until at last she approached the Sun.

When the light grew bright and her eyes could not see, the Autumn winds guided her.

When the light grew hot and scorched her flesh, the Winter Winds braced her. 

She soared until at last, she plunged her walking stick into the burning heights.

quest.6A8ACE8E9B7A056D.quest_desc.original:
As the woman plummeted into the sea, the animals rushed to her knowing she would surely perish without a place to rest.

They took it in turns to dive down into the depths, each attempting to bring up the mud from the bottom. Each boasted of their strengths, claiming they would be the one to reach the bottom. Each failed and upon resurfacing claimed the task to be impossible. 

When hope was nearly lost, the smallest among them came forward and asked to be permitted to try. It made no boast and all doubted its ability, but the other animals stood aside to allow it to try. Through quiet determination, it dove deeper and deeper. It dove until its lungs burned and its limbs ached. It dove until at last, one small paw brushed the mud in the lightless depths. 

Grasping a handful of the mud, it turned and swam to the surface. There, it was greeted with praise when the other animals saw what it held. It spread the mud on Turtle’s back and, knowing that more would be needed, it dove again and again, slowly covering Turtle’s back to build a new land where the woman could rest.

quest.6AF21EDF59FCE0D4.quest_desc.original:
In the time before Time, the Sky People lived high above on islands of clouds. 

They knew no strife for all was changeless. 

They knew no grief for all were eternal. 

Then, something changed. 

One of the Sky People fell from on high. She fell, and fell, and all mourned her as she tumbled through the endless blue sky. The Sky People, seeing her falling, felt that loss cut them deeply. Loss for her and loss for what was and would be no more. 

In their lamentation, their tears fell in great heaving sobs, racing the woman to the place below as if to cushion her fall. 

There, they pooled. There, the oceans rose. And there, the woman landed.

quest.6EBB293F3DDE5EC7.quest_desc.original:
The woman wandered into the East and the animals came with her.

Here, everywhere she walked, the lands bloomed with flowers and lush grasslands. Everywhere she lay down to rest, dense woodlands grew to offer her shade. 

Of the animals who accompanied the woman, some found homes amongst the trees and grasses and were content to stay. 

The East Wind began to blow, gentle and warm, bringing with it Spring and the promise of a new beginning.

quest.7413DDDB64FFAC2A.quest_desc.original:
In what seemed like no time at all, the woman’s explorations led her into the West and the animals came with her.

Here, wetlands gave way to dry brushland and stifling deserts. The woman found travel difficult and though she grew tired faster, places to take rest were fewer and farther between. Food grew scarce and the days grew hard. 

The woman watched the animals cache food when it was available. Doing as they did, she could eat when none could be found. Seeing that shelter was scarce, she made her own that she could carry and move with her.

Of the animals who accompanied the woman, some took to the air and made homes in the trees and were content to stay.

The West Wind began to blow, dry and harsh, bringing with it Autumn and the promise of challenges ahead.

quest.799839991CC1DDF9.quest_desc.original:
It is no small wonder that ancient stories still resonate with us. 

They speak to something universal; an unbroken thread reaching back through time. A thread that binds us all together through shared experience and common plights. Memories linger, stories repeat, and we find their echoes in our day-to-day, in the stories we tell of our lives. 

Here, we find ourselves at the end. And with every end, a new story takes root.  

What threads will we weave for those who will come after us?

quest.7B6DF86E979614F4.quest_desc.original:
Having sat for some time in thought and preparation, the woman stepped out from her snow-bound home.

She looked to the East and implored the gentle winds of Spring to raise her. 

She looked to the South and beseeched the warm winds of Summer to protect her.

She looked to the West and urged the dry winds of Autumn to guide her.

She looked to the North and pleaded that the cold winds of Winter brace her.

The Four Winds came at her call and uplifted her into the heavens. Soaring higher than even the birds dare, joy and elation swelled in her heart.
